greeting_text: Here's what's happening in your world this week!

items.headline: Something Big Is Moving in the Ocean
body: Scientists recently detected something unusual deep in the Pacific Ocean — a massive underwater mountain that's actually a volcano moving across the seafloor! This isn't a tiny shift; researchers say it's one of the biggest geological movements they've ever witnessed underwater.
Imagine a mountain walking around on the ocean floor, slowly but surely. That's basically what's happening right now, miles beneath the waves where no one can see it.
items.analogy: Think of it like a giant turtle slowly swimming underwater — except the turtle is as big as three football fields stacked on top of each other!

items.headline: A Country Planted Millions of Trees in Just One Year
body: One small country in Africa just broke a world record: they planted over 10 million trees in a single year! The goal is to bring back forests that were cut down long ago and help fix climate change.
Trees are like the Earth's lungs — they breathe in carbon dioxide (the stuff that makes the planet warmer) and breathe out clean oxygen for us to breathe.

items.title: There's a lake that looks like it's boiling
items.body: In a faraway place called Dallol, Ethiopia, there's a lake filled with acid so hot and strange that nothing can survive in it — not even bacteria! It looks like a colorful soup bubbling away on an alien planet.

items.title: Some frogs freeze solid in winter and come back to life
items.body: Wood frogs in Alaska and Canada actually freeze completely during winter. Their bodies turn into little frog-cicles! When spring arrives, they thaw out and hop away like nothing happened.

items.title: Honey never spoils — ancient honey is still edible
items.body: Archaeologists have found honey in Egyptian tombs that's over 3,000 years old and still good to eat! Honey's special makeup keeps it fresh forever, as long as it's kept dry.

footer.tagline: Made with love by Dad & Claude 🤖❤️
